D2 Vision L3 Apply Reference

An Orange Under Any Light

toggle between the raw sensor and the perceiving brain and watch one orange survive three different lights

Colour constancy is the brain estimating the illuminant and dividing it out, so an object keeps its colour as the light around it changes.

The same orange sits under daylight, tungsten, and cool shade. Toggle to the raw-sensor view to see the three different colours it actually reflects, then back to the perceiving view where the brain holds it orange.
